def get_user_transactions(self, userId):
        cursor = self.conn.cursor()
        cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM Transactions WHERE userId = '{str(userId)}'")
        rows = cursor.fetchall()
findings.remediation: Review this AI usage against the internal AI policy. Ensure the provider/library is approved, data handling is documented, and usage is logged.
findings.description: SQL query built with an f-string passed directly to cursor.execute() — user-controlled input without parameterisation enables SQL injection; use parameterised queries with ? placeholders.
findings.llm_reason: User-controlled input flows into SQL query.

threats.title: Cross Context Injection in main.py
threats.description: Conversation history or session memory injected into the active prompt context without a trust boundary — a poisoned prior turn can carry instructions into future interactions (cross-context injection). Validate and sanitise stored context before reuse.
mitigations: Review the affected code path and document whether the AI behavior is intentional and controlled.
Add boundary checks, logging, and policy controls around this AI interaction.

Add boundary checks, logging, and policy controls around this AI interaction.

threats.title: Hardcoded Credential Db in transaction_db.py
threats.description: Plaintext credentials inserted directly into the database — passwords must be hashed (bcrypt/argon2) before storage; never store or commit plaintext passwords in seed data or fixtures.
mitigations: Move credentials to a managed secret store and rotate any exposed values.
Prevent secret-bearing values from entering prompts, logs, and model requests.
